subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
Celestia includes the following components:

- **1 airship miniature** (cardboard 3D airship)
- **4 dice** (2 standard dice showing hazards, 2 additional dice for later cities)
- **8 city tiles** arranged in a line, numbered 1–8
- **Treasure cards** of varying values (1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 12, 15, 20 VP)
- **Equipment cards**: Lightning Rods, Foghorns, Compasses, and Jetpacks
- **Power cards**: special ability cards
- **1 Captain pawn** to indicate the current captain

Treasure cards are sorted by value and placed face down on the corresponding city tiles.

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Arrange the **8 city tiles** in a line from left to right (City 1 through City 8).
2. Place the corresponding **treasure card stacks** on each city tile:
   - **Cities 1–2**: Low-value treasures (1–2 VP)
   - **Cities 3–4**: Medium treasures (2–4 VP)
   - **Cities 5–6**: High treasures (6–9 VP)
   - **Cities 7–8**: Legendary treasures (12–20 VP)
3. Place the **airship** at the starting position before City 1.
4. Place all **dice** near the board.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Shuffle the **equipment and power card deck** together.
2. Deal **6 cards** to each player as their starting hand.
3. Place all **player pawns** inside the airship.
4. The most adventurous player (or a random player) becomes the **first Captain** and takes the Captain pawn.
5. Players keep their hand of cards **hidden** from other players.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Celestia is a **push-your-luck** game where players are passengers aboard a magical airship traveling through the clouds to increasingly valuable cities.

Each turn, the **Captain** rolls dice to determine what hazards the ship faces. Before the Captain attempts to overcome the hazards, each **passenger** must decide: stay on the ship or jump off and collect treasure from the current city.

The further the ship travels, the more valuable the treasure — but if the Captain fails, **everyone still on board gets nothing** and returns to the ship for the next round. The first player to accumulate **50 victory points** in treasure wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each journey follows this sequence:

### 1. Roll the Dice
The **Captain** rolls the dice. The number of dice depends on which city the ship is approaching:
- **Cities 1–4**: Roll 2 dice
- **Cities 5–6**: Roll 3 dice
- **Cities 7–8**: Roll 4 dice

Each die face shows a hazard: **lightning bolts**, **fog clouds**, **birds**, or **blank** (no hazard). The Captain must have equipment cards matching each hazard shown.

### 2. Passengers Decide
After seeing the dice results, each **non-Captain passenger** simultaneously decides:
- **Stay on the airship**: You continue the journey, hoping for bigger rewards.
- **Disembark**: Leave the ship and draw a treasure card from the **current city** (the last city reached).

Disembarking players collect treasure safely and sit out until the next journey.

### 3. Captain Resolves
The Captain must now play equipment cards matching every hazard rolled:
- **Lightning bolt** → Play a **Lightning Rod**
- **Fog** → Play a **Foghorn**
- **Birds** → Play a **Compass**

If the Captain plays all required cards, the **ship advances** to the next city. If the Captain **cannot or chooses not to**, the ship crashes — all remaining passengers (including the Captain) get **nothing**.

### 4. Next Stop or New Journey
If the ship advances, a new round begins at the next city with the same Captain. The Captain role rotates only when a journey ends (success at City 8 or a crash).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Equipment Cards
- **Lightning Rod**: Cancels one lightning bolt hazard.
- **Foghorn**: Cancels one fog hazard.
- **Compass**: Cancels one bird hazard.
- **Jetpack**: Allows a passenger to disembark to the **next city** instead of the current one (played during the decision phase).

### Power Cards
Special cards that break the rules in various ways:
- **Turbo**: The Captain can play this to automatically succeed, regardless of dice.
- **Ejector Seat**: Force another player to disembark before they choose.
- **Return Flight**: Retrieve a previously played equipment card.
- Other power cards may modify dice, steal treasure, or alter the journey.

### Bluffing
The Captain can see the dice and knows whether they have the right cards. They may **bluff confidence** to keep passengers on board (hoping they'll crash and lose nothing while the Captain also loses nothing). Conversely, a Captain may appear worried to encourage passengers to jump off early, letting the Captain continue alone to bigger treasures.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Captain Rotation
The Captain role passes **clockwise** after each complete journey (whether the ship crashes or reaches a city where all passengers disembark).

### Empty Ship
If all passengers disembark and only the Captain remains, the Captain may continue alone, collecting treasure personally if they succeed.

### Drawing Cards
At the **start of each new journey**, all players aboard draw back up to **6 cards** (if below 6). The draw pile is reshuffled from discards when empty.

### City 8 (Final Destination)
If the ship reaches **City 8**, the Captain automatically succeeds. All remaining passengers collect the most valuable treasures.

### Multiple Treasures at One City
If a city's treasure stack runs out, players who disembark there get nothing from that city.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
### Winning Condition
The game ends immediately when a player **reaches 50 or more victory points** in collected treasure cards. That player wins.

### Treasure Values
Treasure cards have fixed point values:
- **City 1**: 1 VP treasures
- **City 2**: 1–2 VP treasures
- **City 3**: 2–3 VP treasures
- **City 4**: 3–4 VP treasures
- **City 5**: 4–6 VP treasures
- **City 6**: 6–9 VP treasures
- **City 7**: 9–12 VP treasures
- **City 8**: 15–20 VP treasures

### Variant: Fixed Rounds
For a shorter game, play a **set number of journeys** (e.g., 8–10) and the player with the most VP at the end wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Don't get greedy early**: Cities 3–4 offer decent treasures with manageable risk. Jumping off here consistently builds a safe VP lead.
- **Watch the Captain's hand size**: A Captain with few cards likely cannot overcome tough rolls. Consider disembarking.
- **Read the dice**: Count the hazards. If 3 different hazards appear, the Captain needs 3 specific cards — that's tough.
- **Save power cards**: Turbo and Jetpack are extremely powerful. Use them at high-value cities for maximum impact.
- **Remember who has what**: Track which equipment cards other players have used. This helps you assess the Captain's odds.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Conservative accumulator**: Consistently disembark at Cities 3–4 to build steady VP. Let greedy players crash while you quietly accumulate.
- **High-roller**: Stay on the ship as long as possible, targeting Cities 6–8. One or two big scores can win the game outright.
- **Bluff Captain**: When you're Captain, act confident even without the right cards. If passengers stay and you crash, nobody scores — but you lose nothing extra compared to them.
- **Card counting**: Track which equipment cards have been played. If many Lightning Rods are gone, lightning results become much more dangerous.
- **Captain timing**: Plan your big pushes for when **you** are Captain, since you control whether the ship continues and can guarantee success with the right cards.
- **Ejector Seat politics**: Use the Ejector Seat on the leading player to deny them treasure from high-value cities.
